intake.risk_posture: medium; preserve behavior and route semantics, allow limited visual drift toward Timeline/D-zone/Database canon
intake.harness_policy: do not edit AGENTS.md or plugin skills in this tranche; queue learning/lint-law follow-ups if evidence appears
allowed_areas: focused tests/docs for the approved slice
protected_areas: database detail modal internals unless touched by a selected shared primitive
validation_commands: focused npx vitest run commands for touched areas
intake.stop_condition: stop when tranche is validated, same-concept scope expansion appears, validation fails twice, or product/design decision is needed

focus_areas.name: Impeccable extract/design-system normalization
focus_areas.evidence: User requested @impeccable extract; PRODUCT/DESIGN define Timeline and D-zone as canon and Database browse conventions as settled.
focus_areas.next: Scout/Judge first shared token or primitive slice.

focus_areas.name: Tailwind/CSS normalization
focus_areas.evidence: Target surfaces use overlapping slate/amber/radius/motion values through CSS vars, Tailwind arbitrary values, RGB, and OKLCH.

focus_areas.name: React/component composition
focus_areas.evidence: Timeline controls, D-zone alert switcher, and database controls repeat related product-control concepts with page-local markup.

focus_areas.name: UI/a11y
focus_areas.evidence: Focus, reduced motion, custom controls, card overlays, and responsive overflow must be preserved during migration.
focus_areas.next: Include in Judge invariants and browser checks.

focus_areas.name: Architecture/module boundaries
focus_areas.evidence: Shared UI vocabulary is desirable, but database card layout and Timeline/D-zone semantics should stay local.

focus_areas.name: Complexity hotspots
focus_areas.evidence: Complexity scan flags D-zone history, Timeline banner artwork, and database browse render-derived work inside or adjacent to scope.

candidates.title: Shared product-surface CSS tokens
candidates.concept_blast_radius: src/index.css or shared style entry; src/features/database/database.css; src/pages/timeline/timeline.css; src/pages/d-zone/d-zone.css
candidates.evidence: Implemented in W1. Added `--ui-*` product-surface tokens in src/index.css; mapped D-zone local vars to them; migrated Timeline and Database motion/focus/control styling to token references.
candidates.next: Implemented for the first tranche; future slices can migrate more component-level uses.

candidates.title: Shared compact chip/segmented control styling
candidates.concept_blast_radius: src/ui/filters/FilterChipButton.tsx; possible new shared primitive; Timeline price filter; D-zone alert switcher; database controls
candidates.evidence: User named compact chip/segmented control; prior audit found similar controls in all three surfaces.
candidates.next: Needs Judge to avoid mixing different interaction semantics.

candidates.title: Shared section divider/header
candidates.concept_blast_radius: Timeline section headers; database browse row/header areas; possible src/ui primitive
candidates.evidence: Timeline has duplicate section heading shapes.
candidates.next: Queue unless first slice is smaller and safer.

candidates.title: Shared metadata token/line
candidates.concept_blast_radius: Timeline event/banner metadata; database card meta/stat areas; possible src/ui primitive
candidates.evidence: Repeated uppercase micro-label and compact metadata rows.
candidates.next: Needs Scout on intent differences before extraction.

candidates.title: Compact media action tile
candidates.concept_blast_radius: D-zone relic/monster buttons; database square-art/icon cards; src/ui/cards or feature adapters
candidates.evidence: Similar image well plus action target, but database hybrid cards are feature-owned.
candidates.next: Only implement if it does not globalize database card behavior.

candidates.title: D-zone history/browser complexity cleanup
candidates.evidence: Complexity scan flags long function, deep nesting, and repeated search in loop.
candidates.next: Later characterization unless Scout shows easy root fix.

candidates.title: Timeline banner artwork complexity cleanup
candidates.evidence: Complexity scan flags large file, branch-heavy, effects, inline style, arbitrary classes, outline-none.
candidates.next: Later characterization.

candidates.title: Database browse render-derived work
candidates.evidence: Performance scan flags render-derived collection work.
candidates.next: Queue unless directly relevant to selected UI primitive.

candidates.title: Neutral shared scrollbar utility
candidates.evidence: Implemented in W1. `.database-scrollbar` was a global utility with a database-specific name and was used by Timeline and D-zone; W1 added `.ui-scrollbar`, preserved the legacy alias, and migrated non-database/shared consumers.
candidates.next: Database detail internals keep the compatibility alias because they are protected in this goal.

candidates.title: Timeline active/upcoming/archive section renderer
candidates.evidence: Event and banner sections both partition active/upcoming/ended and render archive sections.
candidates.next: Queue for a later Timeline-local simplification slice.

candidates.title: Timeline banner tag/pricing composition helper
candidates.evidence: Banner hero and drawer duplicate tag/pricing composition.
candidates.next: Queue as a low-risk Timeline-local helper slice.

candidates.title: Shared compact disclosure row
candidates.concept_blast_radius: Timeline archive section; D-zone archive note/year toggles; possible src/ui primitive
candidates.evidence: Timeline and D-zone both use chevron disclosure rows with `aria-expanded`.
candidates.next: Needs Judge to confirm same interaction intent before extraction.

candidates.title: D-zone countdown helper
candidates.evidence: Current and history pages duplicate countdown logic.
candidates.next: Queue as a small D-zone behavior-preserving helper slice.

candidates.title: D-zone alert switcher component extraction
candidates.evidence: Alert switcher is a coherent local segmented control and may later normalize toward shared chip controls.
candidates.next: Queue after shared control token/primitive direction is clearer.

candidates.title: D-zone history browser subcomponent split
candidates.evidence: History browser mixes modal behavior, focus, search, archive note, year groups, and season buttons.
candidates.next: Queue archive note/year group split after characterization.

candidates.title: D-zone relic and monster button extraction
candidates.evidence: Relic and monster buttons are coherent media-action tile concepts with existing collapsed-accessibility tests.
candidates.next: Queue locally before considering global media tile extraction C5.

candidates.title: Database card title and prioritized image primitive
candidates.evidence: Three database card types repeat title markup and prioritized image count.
candidates.next: Queue as a low-risk database internal simplification.

candidates.title: Database browse sort label config
candidates.evidence: Awakener and wheel sort label/direction helpers live inside the browse orchestration file.
candidates.next: Queue as a behavior-preserving config extraction.

candidates.title: Database mobile filter group extraction
candidates.evidence: Awakener and wheel filters duplicate mobile open state, ids, toggle, panel, and desktop rows.
candidates.next: Queue with UI/a11y checks for disclosure semantics.

candidates.title: Split database hybrid card measurement hook
candidates.evidence: `useMeasuredHybridCardMode` owns measurement, resize fallback, context value, and grid rendering in one file.
candidates.next: Queue as internal readability slice.

candidates.title: Database card accessible-name cleanup
candidates.evidence: Overlay button passes both `aria-label` and `aria-labelledby`; `aria-labelledby` wins.
candidates.next: Queue as a small a11y slice once tests can preserve accessible names.

candidates.title: Timeline page orchestration seam
candidates.evidence: TimelinePage combines URL state, data selection, D-zone parsing, detail modal wiring, masthead config, filters, and section layout.
candidates.next: Queue only after smaller Timeline helper slices reduce risk.

candidates.title: D-zone popover provider duplication
candidates.concept_blast_radius: src/pages/DZonePage.tsx; src/pages/DZoneHistoryPage.tsx; D-zone local provider helper
candidates.evidence: Current and history D-zone pages duplicate database popover provider/root wiring.
candidates.next: Queue as D-zone-local architecture cleanup.

maintenance.title: Token usage guardrail
maintenance.next: Consider after first token slice proves naming.

maintenance.title: Design-system documentation update
maintenance.next: Update DESIGN.md or local docs if implementation creates stable names.

tasks.title: Map cross-surface UI normalization candidates
protected_files: database detail modal internals unless shared primitive touches them
tasks.expected_output: Evidence-ranked candidates with concept blast radius, validation ideas, root-fix/patch notes, and maintenance candidates.

evidence: Database scout mapped browse/card/filter candidates and a11y risks.
Timeline scout mapped section headers, segmented controls, meta rows, motion/focus tokens, and the misnamed global scrollbar utility.
D-zone scout mapped local CSS tokens, alert switcher, media buttons, disclosure rows, and history-browser complexity.
Complexity and performance scans added C6, C7, and C8.

tasks.title: Choose first shared-token normalization slice
tasks.expected_output: One root-fix Worker slice for C1 plus C9, with local feature semantics preserved.

evidence: C1 was present in all three scout reports as repeated surface/focus/motion/control tokens.
C9 was explicitly reported by Timeline scout as `.database-scrollbar` used by Timeline and D-zone.
The selected slice avoids package files and database detail modal internals.
receipt.why_root_fix: D-zone already had good local tokens, Timeline and Database repeated the same values, and `.database-scrollbar` was a shared utility with the wrong feature name. Shared `--ui-*` tokens plus a neutral `ui-scrollbar` class remove cross-page reinvention without inventing a large component API.
receipt.what_not_to_change: No database detail modal internals, no Timeline banner drawer behavior, no D-zone wave layout, no dependency files.

tasks.title: Implement shared UI tokens and neutral scrollbar utility
tasks.expected_output: Shared `--ui-*` tokens, D-zone token mapping, Timeline/Database token use, neutral scrollbar migration with compatibility alias.

evidence: Mapped `src/pages/d-zone/d-zone.css` local variables to shared tokens.
Migrated Timeline, Database, D-zone, and shared search scrollbar/motion/control styling to shared token or utility names.
receipt.what_got_simpler: One global SKeyDB product token vocabulary now supplies repeated panel, border, focus, label, radius, motion, and scrollbar values; D-zone keeps local semantic names as aliases; Timeline and Database use shared motion/control tokens instead of duplicating values.
receipt.behavior_preserved: No route, data, modal, card, drawer, or control semantics changed; only styling vocabulary and neutral utility class names changed.
receipt.current_complexity: Three surfaces had separate copies of the same product-surface constants and a global utility named for database.
receipt.expected_complexity_after: Shared constants live in `:root`; feature CSS keeps local layout and semantics.
receipt.root_fix_patch_decision: Root fix for the selected token/utility concept.
receipt.dependency_review: Not needed; no package files changed.
receipt.harness_update_considered: M1 and M2 remain queued after token names prove stable.
validation_run: npx vitest run src/pages/TimelinePage.test.tsx src/pages/timeline/TimelineArchiveSection.test.tsx src/pages/timeline/usePoolCycling.test.tsx src/pages/d-zone/DZoneWaveCard.test.tsx src/pages/DZonePage.test.tsx src/pages/DZoneHistoryPage.test.tsx src/features/database/DatabaseRoutes.test.tsx src/features/database/internal/DatabaseGrid.test.tsx
browser route checks for database, wheels, covenants, timeline, d-zone, and d-zone/history
receipt.risks: Small visual drift from shared label/control tokens; browser checks found no horizontal overflow.

tasks.title: Review W1 token/utility normalization slice
tasks.expected_output: Verdict, validation evidence, candidate register coverage, and next continuation prompt.

evidence: W1 preserved behavior and only changed styling token references plus utility class names.
Candidate register includes C1-C23; C1 and C9 are implemented, remaining in-scope candidates are queued rather than treated as complete.
No dependency files, AGENTS.md, Builder, Collection, or database detail modal internals were edited for this slice.

simplification_check.before: D-zone, Timeline, and Database repeated product-surface values independently; `.database-scrollbar` was a global utility with a feature-specific name.
simplification_check.after: Shared `--ui-*` tokens supply the first product-surface vocabulary; feature CSS keeps layout and semantic ownership; `.ui-scrollbar` is the neutral shared utility with compatibility alias.
simplification_check.net: Reduced token duplication and naming drift without creating a large shared component abstraction.

governance_check.focus_area_coverage: tranche covers extract/Tailwind token normalization; React/component, architecture, a11y, and complexity follow-ups remain queued
governance_check.root_fix_patch: root fix for selected token/utility concept
governance_check.characterization: existing tests plus browser checks sufficient for styling-token migration

validation: npm run lint passed after final code changes
npm run build:quiet passed after final code changes

final_audit.summary: First tranche implemented shared UI tokens and neutral scrollbar utility. Broad cross-surface normalization remains active because component, complexity, and a11y candidates are queued.
validation: browser checks passed for touched routes
